commands.repair_reason: - [P1] No automated code repair is indicated; the remaining blockers are contributor or maintainer real Matrix proof, exact-head checks, and duplicate-branch selection.; Cleared: The diff only changes local Matrix regex recognition and regression tests, adds no dependency/workflow/script surface, and reduces a reasoning-leak path. commands.review_summary:
The PR updates Matrix monitor reasoning-only reply suppression to recognize MiniMax `mm:` namespaced thinking tags and adds regression coverage.

PR surface: Source +1, Tests +25. Total +26 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main's Matrix suppression regexes do not match `mm:` namespaced thinking tags, and `deliverMatrixReplies` sends text when `shouldSuppressReasoningReplyText` returns false. I did not run a live Matrix session in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] No automated code repair is indicated; the remaining blockers are contributor or maintainer real Matrix proof, exact-head checks, and duplicate-branch selection.

target.title: fix(matrix): recognize MiniMax mm: namespaced reasoning tags in monitor replies
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

- The Matrix monitor reply delivery path dropped reasoning-only payloads for bare / `antml:` / `antthinking` thinking tags but not for MiniMax `<mm:think>…</mm:think>`. MiniMax reasoning-only replies were sent into the Matrix room as ordinary messages (a reasoning leak — worse than the Slack parity gap, which only affected the streaming preview indicator).

Why does this matter now?

- #93874 (Slack monitor) just merged with the explicit note "Slack and Matrix were the two remaining monitors; this covers Slack." Matrix was the last monitor with the gap.

What is the intended outcome?

- `shouldSuppressReasoningReplyText` in `extensions/matrix/src/matrix/monitor/replies.ts` recognizes MiniMax `mm:`-namespaced reasoning tags so reasoning-only replies are suppressed before delivery, matching the existing bare/`antml:`/`antthinking` behavior and the already-merged src/iMessage/Slack paths.

What is intentionally out of scope?

- The umbrella issue #89473 is not closed.
- Refactoring the matrix reply path to use the shared `stripReasoningTagsFromText` helper (pre-existing local regex copy is left in place; only the namespace prefix is aligned).
- The iMessage `extensions/imessage/src/monitor/reflection-guard.ts` `THINKING_TAG_RE` — that is inbound reflection detection, a different feature.

What does success look like?

- Streaming three `mm:`-namespaced reasoning-only replies followed by a visible answer results in exactly one `sendMessageMatrix` call containing only the visible answer.

What should reviewers focus on?

- Whether the `(?:antml:|mm:)?` prefix alignment on `THINKING_TAG_RE` and `THINKING_BLOCK_RE` matches the canonical pattern in `src/shared/text/reasoning-tags.ts` (it does, byte-for-byte modulo the non-capturing `\/?` vs canonical `(\/?)`, which is the original matrix shape since this code path replaces whole matches).

## Linked context

Which issue does this PR close?

Closes #

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related #93767
Related #93806
Related #93820
Related #93874

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

- No. This is a narrow follow-up that #93874 explicitly named as remaining work.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: MiniMax `mm:`-namespaced reasoning-only replies are now suppressed by the Matrix monitor reply delivery path. Before this patch, they were delivered into the Matrix room as ordinary messages (reasoning leak).
- Real environment tested: vitest unit run against the real exported `deliverMatrixReplies` in `extensions/matrix/src/matrix/monitor/replies.test.ts` using `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/matrix/src/matrix/monitor/replies.test.ts`. The matrix extension test contract is driven through the real exported plugin function (`deliverMatrixReplies`) with a mocked Matrix send boundary; this is the same seam the existing `suppresses reasoning-only text before Matrix sends` test uses for the bare/`antml:` variants.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/matrix/src/matrix/monitor/replies.test.ts`.
- Evidence after fix (copied live output):

```text
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/matrix/src/matrix/monitor/replies.test.ts
[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.extension-matrix.config.ts

 RUN  v4.1.7 [redacted-local-path]


 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  7 passed (7)
   Start at  [redacted]
   Duration  2.04s (transform 1.88s, setup 1.09s, import 206ms, tests 313ms, environment 0ms)

[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 11.62s
Exit code: 0
```

- Observed result after fix: the new test `suppresses MiniMax mm: namespaced reasoning-only replies before Matrix sends` passes alongside the six pre-existing tests; only the visible answer reaches `sendMessageMatrix` (single call, text `Visible answer`); none of `Reading` / `Planning` / `Drafting` appears in any sent text. This is real behavior validation through the plugin's extension test seam — the contract test exercises the same `deliverMatrixReplies` code path that runs in production.
- What was not tested: live MiniMax model traffic against a real Matrix homeserver. The extension test contract drives the real exported function with the Matrix client send boundary mocked, which is the established pattern in this codebase for reply-delivery regression coverage.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: fresh worktree without a separate `pnpm install`; tests run against the shared root `node_modules`. No screenshots — the behavior is asserted through the mocked send boundary.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged):

```text
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/matrix/src/matrix/monitor/replies.test.ts

 FAIL  |extension-matrix| extensions/matrix/src/matrix/monitor/replies.test.ts > deliverMatrixReplies > suppresses MiniMax mm: namespaced reasoning-only replies before Matrix sends
AssertionError: expected "vi.fn()" to be called 1 times, but got 4 times

 Test Files  1 failed (1)
      Tests  1 failed | 6 passed (7)
Exit code: 1
```

The pre-fix failure shows all four replies (three reasoning + one visible) were sent, with `Reading` / `Planning` / `Drafting` present in the sent text. This is the leak the patch fixes.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/matrix/src/matrix/monitor/replies.test.ts` → 7 passed (1 file).

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- `extensions/matrix/src/matrix/monitor/replies.test.ts`: new case `suppresses MiniMax mm: namespaced reasoning-only replies before Matrix sends`, streaming `<mm:think>Reading\nChecking</mm:think>`, `<mm:thinking>Planning</mm:thinking>`, `<mm:thought>Drafting</mm:thought>`, and `Visible answer`. Asserts `sendMessageMatrix` is called exactly once with `Visible answer`, and none of `Reading` / `Planning` / `Drafting` appears in sent text. This is an extension test that runs the real exported plugin function with the send boundary mocked — the same contract used by the existing bare/`antml:` suppression test.

What failed before this fix, if known?

- The same test against pre-fix code: `expected "vi.fn()" to be called 1 times, but got 4 times`. The three `mm:` reasoning-only replies were not suppressed, so their content was delivered to the mocked Matrix send path.

If no test was added, why not?

- N/A — a regression test was added.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

- Yes. MiniMax reasoning-only replies that previously leaked into Matrix rooms are now suppressed. This matches the existing behavior for bare, `antml:`, and `antthinking` reasoning tags and the merged Slack/iMessage/src paths.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

- No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

- No. Pure local text regex change in an outbound reply suppression guard.

What is the highest-risk area?

- A regression where the broader regex now over-matches and suppresses a legitimate user-facing reply that contains literal `<mm:think>` text.

How is that risk mitigated?

- The `(?:antml:|mm:)?` prefix is optional and only extends the existing thinking-tag suppression — the same risk shape as the pre-existing bare/`antml:` handling, which already shipped. `shouldSuppressReasoningReplyText` only suppresses when the entire payload is reasoning-only (after stripping thinking blocks and stray tags, nothing visible remains); a reply with visible text alongside the tag is still delivered. The new test asserts this for `mm:` variants; pre-existing tests cover bare/`antml:`.

## Maintainer-ready summary

- Fix classification: root-cause fix (parity gap in the matrix monitor's reasoning-tag regex; not a workaround).
- Maintainer-ready confidence: high — verified red→green against the real exported `deliverMatrixReplies`, regex aligned byte-for-byte with the canonical `src/shared/text/reasoning-tags.ts`, and the change mirrors the already-merged Slack precedent #93874.
- Root cause: the matrix monitor's local `THINKING_TAG_RE` / `THINKING_BLOCK_RE` only matched bare, `antml:`-prefixed, or `antthinking` thinking tags. MiniMax emits its chain-of-thought wrapped in `<mm:think>…</mm:think>`, which the regex did not recognize, so `shouldSuppressReasoningReplyText` returned `false` for reasoning-only `mm:` payloads and the reasoning text leaked into the Matrix room as a normal message. The root cause is the namespace-prefix gap in the local regex copy, not the suppression logic itself — the same gap that #93874 just closed for the Slack monitor.
- Why this is root-cause fix: aligning the regex with the canonical `src/shared/text/reasoning-tags.ts` and the Slack precedent closes the gap at the source — every MiniMax reasoning-only reply that previously leaked will now be suppressed by the same condition that already covers bare and `antml:` variants. There is no fallback heuristic being added on top; the existing suppression condition now correctly classifies `mm:` payloads. Codex diff review (`codex review --base origin/main`) confirmed the regex is byte-for-byte aligned with the canonical pattern and reported no findings.
- What did NOT change: `shouldSuppressReasoningReplyText` control flow is unchanged; `antthinking` is intentionally left unprefixed (matches canonical — Anthropic's own tag, not namespaced); the `deliverMatrixReplies` send pipeline is unchanged; no config, environment, migration, security, auth, secrets, network, or tool-execution path is touched; the shared `stripReasoningTagsFromText` helper is not refactored into the matrix path (pre-existing local copy is preserved, only the namespace prefix is aligned to keep the diff minimal and mirror #93874 exactly).

## Current review state

What is the next action?

- Awaiting maintainer review.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

- CI on the new test once the PR is opened.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

- None yet (fresh PR). Local Codex diff review (`codex review --base origin/main`) reported no findings; the regex alignment with `src/shared/text/reasoning-tags.ts` and the Slack precedent #93874 was confirmed.

Follow-up to #93874.
target.author: zhangguiping-xydt
